name: AI Newsletter - RSS Feed Collection (Every 4 Hours)

nodes.name: Cron Trigger (Every 4 Hours)
nodes.notes: Triggers the RSS collection workflow every 4 hours. Adjust the interval as needed. If the workflow takes longer than expected, consider increasing the interval or adding execution locking.

nodes.name: RSS - Anthropic Blog
nodes.notes: Reads the Anthropic Blog RSS feed. This feed URL is approximated and may need adjustment if Anthropic changes their feed endpoint. Consider adding a retry mechanism or fallback.

nodes.name: RSS - OpenAI Blog
nodes.notes: Reads the OpenAI Blog RSS feed. continueOnFail is enabled so that one feed failure does not break the entire workflow.

nodes.name: RSS - Google DeepMind
nodes.notes: Reads the Google DeepMind Blog RSS feed.

nodes.name: RSS - Hugging Face Blog
nodes.notes: Reads the Hugging Face Blog RSS feed.

nodes.name: RSS - TechCrunch AI
nodes.notes: Reads the TechCrunch AI category RSS feed.

nodes.name: RSS - The Verge AI
nodes.notes: Reads The Verge AI RSS feed.

nodes.name: RSS - VentureBeat AI
nodes.notes: Reads the VentureBeat AI category RSS feed.

nodes.name: RSS - Ars Technica
nodes.notes: Reads the Ars Technica Technology Lab RSS feed (covers AI topics).

nodes.name: RSS - MIT Tech Review
nodes.notes: Reads the MIT Technology Review RSS feed.

nodes.name: RSS - NVIDIA AI Blog
nodes.notes: Reads the NVIDIA AI Blog RSS feed.

nodes.name: RSS - AWS AI Blog
nodes.notes: Reads the AWS Machine Learning Blog RSS feed.

nodes.name: RSS - IEEE Spectrum AI
nodes.notes: Reads the IEEE Spectrum AI topic RSS feed.

nodes.name: Merge Pair 1
nodes.notes: Layer 1: Merges Anthropic Blog + OpenAI Blog

nodes.name: Merge Pair 2
nodes.notes: Layer 1: Merges Google DeepMind + Hugging Face Blog

nodes.name: Merge Pair 3
nodes.notes: Layer 1: Merges TechCrunch AI + The Verge AI

nodes.name: Merge Pair 4
nodes.notes: Layer 1: Merges VentureBeat AI + Ars Technica

nodes.name: Merge Pair 5
nodes.notes: Layer 1: Merges MIT Tech Review + NVIDIA AI Blog

nodes.name: Merge Pair 6
nodes.notes: Layer 1: Merges AWS AI Blog + IEEE Spectrum AI

nodes.name: Merge Quad A
nodes.notes: Layer 2: Merges Pair 1 + Pair 2 (Anthropic, OpenAI, DeepMind, Hugging Face)

nodes.name: Merge Quad B
nodes.notes: Layer 2: Merges Pair 3 + Pair 4 (TechCrunch, The Verge, VentureBeat, Ars Technica)

nodes.name: Merge Quad C
nodes.notes: Layer 2: Merges Pair 5 + Pair 6 (MIT, NVIDIA, AWS, IEEE)

nodes.name: Merge Half AB
nodes.notes: Layer 3: Merges Quad A + Quad B (8 feeds combined)

nodes.name: Merge All Final
nodes.notes: Layer 4 (Final): Merges Half AB + Quad C to produce all 12 feeds in a single stream. Binary merge tree complete: 12 feeds -> 6 pairs -> 3 quads -> 1 half + 1 quad -> 1 final stream.

nodes.name: Normalize & Hash Articles
nodes.notes: PRODUCTION NOTE: This node normalizes all RSS feed items into a consistent schema and computes SHA256 hashes for deduplication. It handles various RSS field naming conventions (link vs url, content vs description, etc.). HTML is stripped from content snippets. Items without URLs are skipped. The crypto module is available in n8n's Code node environment.

nodes.name: Check Empty Result
nodes.notes: Checks if the normalization step returned an empty marker. If true (no articles), the workflow stops gracefully via the warning log branch. If false (articles exist), processing continues to the Google Sheets dedup stage.

nodes.name: Google Sheets - Read Existing Hashes
nodes.notes: SETUP REQUIRED: Replace YOUR_GOOGLE_SHEET_URL_HERE with your actual Google Sheets URL and REPLACE_WITH_CREDENTIAL_ID with your configured Google Sheets OAuth2 credential ID. This node reads all existing hashes from column A of the 'Articles' sheet. The sheet must have a header row with columns: hash, title, url, date, content_snippet, category, processed, collected_at. PERFORMANCE NOTE: For very large sheets (10k+ rows), consider using a database instead of Google Sheets, or read only recent hashes.

nodes.name: Filter Duplicates
nodes.notes: PRODUCTION NOTE: This node performs the core deduplication logic. It builds a Set of existing hashes from Google Sheets and filters out any articles whose hash is already present. Only genuinely new articles pass through. Logs the dedup statistics for monitoring. If all articles are duplicates, returns an empty marker.

nodes.name: Check New Articles Exist
nodes.notes: Checks if the dedup filter found any new articles. If _empty is true (true branch), all articles were duplicates and the workflow logs and stops. If false (false branch), new articles exist and will be appended to Google Sheets.

nodes.name: Google Sheets - Append New Articles
nodes.notes: SETUP REQUIRED: Replace YOUR_GOOGLE_SHEET_URL_HERE and REPLACE_WITH_CREDENTIAL_ID with your actual values. This node appends all new (non-duplicate) articles to the 'Articles' sheet. Columns: hash, title, url, date, content_snippet, category, processed, collected_at. Make sure your Google Sheet has these exact column headers in row 1.

nodes.name: Log Results
nodes.notes: Final logging node. Outputs a summary of how many articles were added. Useful for monitoring workflow health via n8n execution history.

nodes.name: No New Articles - Log
nodes.notes: Logs when no new articles are found. This is a normal outcome if feeds haven't updated since the last run.

nodes.name: No Articles Collected - Log
nodes.notes: WARNING PATH: If no articles are collected at all from 12 feeds, something is likely wrong. Check feed URLs, DNS resolution, and outbound HTTP access from the n8n server.
